news.t: Here are some ETFs State Street likes with recession worries, Fed rate hikes in focus

overview.description: The State Street Ultra Short Term Bond ETF (ULST) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in investment grade fixed income. The fund is an actively managed fund that aims to maximize income and preserve capital using USD-denominated fixed-income securities with maturities ranging from 6 to 18 months and durations of 3 to 6 months. ULST was launched on Oct 9, 2013 and is issued by State Street.

data.title: Here are some ETFs State Street likes with recession worries, Fed rate hikes in focus
data.text: Hello! For this week's ETF Wrap, I spoke with Franklin Templeton's Wylie Tollette on the Federal Reserve's interest-rate decision and caught up with State Street's Michael Arone on his midyear ETF mar...
